steps.title: Identify the cost components
steps.content:
Three components make up the total cost:
  • First hour: flat \$180
  • Additional hours: \$65 per hour beyond the first
  • Safety gear: $w$
Since $x$ is total hours rented, the number of additional hours is $(x - 1)$.

steps.title: Write the equation
steps.content:
$$y = 180 + 65(x - 1) + w$$
Verify with $x = 1$: $y = 180 + 65(0) + w = 180 + w$. Correct — only the flat first-hour fee and safety gear. Verify with $x = 3$: $y = 180 + 65(2) + w = 310 + w$. This is \$180 for hour 1, plus \$65 each for hours 2 and 3, plus safety gear. Correct.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $y = 180 + 65(x - 1) + w$: Correctly charges \$180 flat for the first hour and \$65 for each of the $(x-1)$ additional hours. ✓
  • Choice B — $y = 180 + 65x + w$: Charges $65x$ instead of ${65}(x-1)$, which would charge \$65 for the first hour on top of the \$180 flat fee — overcounting. ✗
  • Choice C — $y = 180 + 65 + w$: A constant — doesn't depend on $x$ at all. Clearly wrong for varying rental lengths. ✗
  • Choice D — $y = 65(x - 1) + w$: Omits the \$180 first-hour charge entirely. ✗
That confirms A is correct.

explanation:
• Choice A — $y = 180 + 65(x - 1) + w$: \$180 flat for hour 1, plus \$65 for each of $(x-1)$ additional hours, plus safety gear. At $x=1$: $y = 180 + w$. ✓
  • Choice B — $y = 180 + 65x + w$: Charges \$65 for all $x$ hours including the first, double-billing the first hour on top of the \$180. ✗
  • Choice C — $y = 180 + 65 + w$: Constant; ignores $x$ entirely. ✗
  • Choice D — $y = 65(x - 1) + w$: Forgets the \$180 first-hour flat charge. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
